adjudication_method: Each classification disagreement was resolved against recovered current or historical behavior. When the exact trace-time body was unavailable, successor behavior was not treated as proof and the final classification is needs_context.

records.adjudication_rationale: Recovered historical behavior only derives target identifiers from an in-memory event; it does not dispatch, persist, or hand data to an external service.
The recovered pre-removal body only formats a decision reason from existing findings and performs no I/O or observable handoff.
The exact parity-consumer body is absent from current and recovered history; a successor's private snapshot bookkeeping does not prove the trace-time behavior.
The record anchor is a constant-valued adapter identifier property with no I/O or handoff; mixed sibling functions in the grouped message do not change the anchored behavior.
Complete current and originating historical implementations log the observation count before the external client call, so the no-logging premise does not reproduce.
The complete function has logged the terminal transition and emitted telemetry before the event handoff since its introduction.
The exact callback body is unavailable; a successor performs an event-driven state transition, but that lineage alone cannot establish the scanned draft's behavior.
The reported method is the adapter telemetry sink abstraction itself; requiring that sink to log or emit telemetry would be circular.
Recovered historical behavior only extracts target identifiers into an immutable in-memory set and performs no event or package handoff.
The reported facade defines no such method, and the exact historical implementation was not recovered; a successor in the implementation module is insufficient to classify the trace-time body.
The exact callable body is unavailable; similar lineage contains a subscription handoff, but that does not prove the behavior of this scanned draft.
The function is an internal projection helper invoked by the actual event handler; it parses details and mutates preview state but does not receive or dispatch the event.
The exact function body is unavailable; a related implementation subscribes to a cloud stream, but classification of this draft cannot be inferred from that successor.
The exact mount callback is absent from current and committed source; a later callback that only populates local widgets does not establish this draft's behavior.
The matching current and originating historical implementations already log the postprocess scoring boundary before delegating to score emission.
Recovered historical behavior classifies dictionaries and constructs typed values in memory; it neither consumes a runtime callback nor crosses an external boundary.
The available implementation is an imported internal projection helper called by the actual event handler; the recorded location is an attribution artifact, not an independent boundary.
Complete current and originating historical implementations log the observation count immediately before the external telemetry client call.
The recovered historical helper only accumulates normalized values into in-memory counters and does not emit, publish, persist, or dispatch anything.
The record anchor scans mapping keys, normalizes a string, and returns it; actual adapter entry points included in the grouped message do not alter that anchored helper's behavior.
The record anchor is a constant adapter source identifier property with no I/O or state transition; mixed I/O siblings in the grouped message are separate behaviors.
The exact recovery-consumption method is unavailable; a successor validates and clears internal retry state, but that does not establish the scanned method's implementation.
Recovered historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback handing review state into mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.
Recovered historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback handing review state into mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.
Recovered historical source shows a post-refresh lifecycle callback handing review state into mounted UI rendering without logging or telemetry.
The exact parity-consumer symbol is absent from current and recovered history; a successor's private read, compare, and delete behavior cannot prove the original body.
The matching current and originating historical implementations log the score-emission boundary immediately before the external client call.
